title: Full-scale evaluation of BSD and A-CFG on Countdown-500 × 3 seeds
description: All BSD and A-CFG claims are on n=16 pilot data. Run Countdown-500 with seeds {42, 123, 456} for both methods. This is the single most important gap in the paper.

title: Full-scale GSM8K evaluation (n≥100)
description: A-CFG's 'cross-benchmark generalization' claim rests on 6/16 vs 4/16 correct. Run GSM8K-1319 for A-CFG and vanilla at minimum. The 95% CI currently includes substantial negative effects.

title: Separate pilot and full-scale results with explicit scale qualifiers
description: Every pilot result (n=16) must be clearly labeled with '(pilot, n=16)' in table headers, section titles, and abstract. Currently the abstract mixes validated and unvalidated claims without distinction.

title: Reframe contributions around evidence strength
description: Primary contribution: DMI (validated). Secondary: negative results design space mapping (validated). Tertiary: BSD and A-CFG as promising directions (pilot only). The current framing treats BSD and A-CFG as co-equal contributions with DMI.

title: Ablation studies at n=100
description: BSD k_frac and A-CFG schedule ablations at n=16 cannot distinguish noise from signal. Re-run the most important ablations (BSD k_frac, A-CFG schedule type) on Countdown-100 to confirm findings.

title: Pilot-to-full-scale shrinkage meta-analysis
description: Quantify pilot-to-full-scale effect size changes across all 4 iterations. This calibrates expectations for BSD and A-CFG at full scale and provides a methodological contribution.

title: Deep DMI analysis as a standalone contribution
description: DMI is the strongest validated finding. Conduct deep analysis: per-token-type impact, per-step contribution, α sweep [0.0-1.0], cross-model validation on LLaDA-8B. This could anchor the paper even if BSD/A-CFG fail at full scale.

title: Address BSD underperformance vs DMI
description: BSD (6.2%) < DMI (12.5%) on the same pilot benchmark, yet BSD is framed as a 'generalization' of DMI. The paper must directly confront this: why does the generalization underperform the special case? Is it OOD input distribution? The k_frac sensitivity suggests the belief phase actively hurts.

title: Clarify A-CFG novelty vs reproduction
description: A-CFG is a direct application of arXiv 2505.20199 to Dream-7B. The paper should state clearly: 'We apply A-CFG (Arriaga et al., 2025) to Dream-7B; the contribution is the empirical evaluation, not the method itself.' Currently the abstract implies A-CFG is a novel contribution.

title: Entropy-accuracy correlation at larger n
description: The r=0.78 correlation is computed over 16 samples with a binary outcome (2 correct). This estimate is extremely fragile. Re-compute at n≥100 to validate or invalidate the finding.

title: Document plan-execution gap
description: The methodology planned ~70 GPU·h across 4 phases; actual execution was ~10 GPU·h. Add a section or appendix explaining what was and was not completed, and why.

title: Fix Figure 1 inset scale mixing
description: Figure 1 inset mixes full-scale (4.7%, 9.3%) and pilot (6.2%, 12.5%) numbers. Either use only full-scale data or clearly annotate the different scales.

title: Add MetaState comparison or discussion
description: MetaState is the most direct competitor (trained cross-step memory vs BSD's training-free). Either compare experimentally or add a detailed qualitative comparison discussing why direct comparison was not feasible.

title: Reduce hypothesis count
description: 11 hypotheses for a pilot-scale study is excessive. Consolidate to 4-5 core hypotheses to improve narrative focus.

title: Relegate RACFG to appendix
description: RACFG failed completely. Currently it occupies significant space in Sections 3 and 4. Move the RACFG description and results to an appendix and mention it briefly as a negative result in the main text.
